channel.title: NM on Detention Pipeline
channel.description: Recent content in NM on Detention Pipeline

item.title: 287(g) WSO: Curry County Sheriff’s Office (NM)
item.description:
287(g) agreement between ICE and Curry County Sheriff’s Office.

Model: WSO Signed: May 13, 2025 County: Curry County State: NM FIPS: 35009

Source: Prison Policy Initiative appendix table, compiled from ICE data as of Feb 17, 2026.

item.title: ABBA TECHNOLOGIES, INC. — ICE BERNALILLO, NM $736,044
item.description:
ICE contract.

Recipient: ABBA TECHNOLOGIES, INC. Award ID: 70CMSD22FR0000157 Amount: $736,043.86 Description: NIGHT VISION MONOCULAR DEVICES (NVMD) AND THERMAL IMAGING MONOCULAR DEVICES (TI Period: 2022-09-09 to 2023-03-01 Location: BERNALILLO, NM (FIPS: 35001)

item.title: APPLIED RESEARCH ASSOCIATES, INC. — ICE BERNALILLO, NM $1,075,773
item.description:
ICE contract.

Recipient: APPLIED RESEARCH ASSOCIATES, INC. Award ID: 70CTD023C00000003 Amount: $1,075,772.65 Description: IMAGED DOCUMENTS AND EXEMPLARS LIBRARY (IDEAL) 2.0 CONTRACT Period: 2023-09-30 to 2025-01-30 Location: BERNALILLO, NM (FIPS: 35001)

item.title: APPLIED RESEARCH ASSOCIATES, INC. — ICE BERNALILLO, NM $1,502,524
item.description:
ICE contract.

Recipient: APPLIED RESEARCH ASSOCIATES, INC. Award ID: 70CTD024C00000003 Amount: $1,502,524.06 Description: THIS CONTRACT IS FOR IMAGES DOCUMENT AND EXEMPLARS LIBRARY SOFTWARE (IDEAL) 2.0. Period: 2024-09-30 to 2026-03-31 Location: BERNALILLO, NM (FIPS: 35001)

item.title: Cibola County, NM
Cibola County, NM — USDA Distress Profile (3 indicators)
item.description:
USDA Economic Research Service county typology flags Cibola County, NM with 3 distress indicators:

government/corrections-dependent economy; persistent poverty (20%+ poverty rate for 30+ years); low employment ratio

Full flags: Government, Low Employment, Persistent Poverty

This county already has an IGSA detention facility. The combination of existing federal detention relationship + economic distress = high vulnerability to expansion pitch.

Nonmetro: nonmetro

Source: USDA ERS County Typology Codes, 2025 edition

item.description:

item.title: Hidalgo County, NM
Hidalgo County, NM — USDA Distress Profile (3 indicators)
item.description:
USDA Economic Research Service county typology flags Hidalgo County, NM with 3 distress indicators:

government/corrections-dependent economy; losing population; persistent poverty (20%+ poverty rate for 30+ years)

Full flags: Government, Population Loss, Persistent Poverty

This county already has an IGSA detention facility. The combination of existing federal detention relationship + economic distress = high vulnerability to expansion pitch.

Nonmetro: nonmetro

Source: USDA ERS County Typology Codes, 2025 edition

item.title: McKinley County, NM
McKinley County, NM — USDA Distress Profile (4 indicators)
item.description:
USDA Economic Research Service county typology flags McKinley County, NM with 4 distress indicators:

government/corrections-dependent economy; persistent poverty (20%+ poverty rate for 30+ years); low employment ratio; housing stress

Full flags: Government, Housing Stress, Low Employment, Persistent Poverty

This county already has an IGSA detention facility. The combination of existing federal detention relationship + economic distress = high vulnerability to expansion pitch.

Nonmetro: nonmetro

Source: USDA ERS County Typology Codes, 2025 edition

item.title: New Mexico HB 9 (Immigrant Safety Act) — Bill to Ban Public IGSA Agreements
item.description:
New Mexico advanced HB 9 (Immigrant Safety Act) which would ban public bodies from entering ICE detention agreements (IGSAs). This is preemptive legislation responding to the detention expansion pipeline.

States introducing such legislation are states where the pitch is active. NM has 18 IGSA facilities — third highest after TX and CA.

Conversely, states WITHOUT such protections are the open field for Sabot and similar consultants.

Source: RAMM research, NM legislature

item.description:
USDA Economic Research Service county typology flags San Miguel County, NM with 4 distress indicators:

government/corrections-dependent economy; losing population; persistent poverty (20%+ poverty rate for 30+ years); housing stress

Full flags: Government, Housing Stress, Population Loss, Persistent Poverty

This county already has an IGSA detention facility. The combination of existing federal detention relationship + economic distress = high vulnerability to expansion pitch.

Nonmetro: nonmetro

Source: USDA ERS County Typology Codes, 2025 edition
